responseMessageContents.text:
The user is asking me to provide a secure way to share credentials (like GITHUB_TOKEN, RENDER_API_KEY, etc.) within the IVX project. They want a secure "box" or mechanism to share credentials.

Looking at the conversation context, the user has been trying to fix the Render auto-deploy pipeline, and the blocker has been missing credentials (empty GITHUB_TOKEN, etc.). Now they're asking for a secure way to share credentials.

Let me think about what they need:

1. A secure way to store and share credentials between the backend and deployment pipeline
2. The existing `requestEnvs` tool is the proper mechanism for collecting environment variables from the user

But the user is asking me to "provide security to boxe to share credentials now" - this likely means they want me to set up the proper secure credential input mechanism.

The best approach here is to use the `requestEnvs` tool to collect the missing credentials (RENDER_API_KEY, RENDER_SERVICE_ID, GITHUB_TOKEN, etc.) that are needed for the deployment pipeline to work.

Let me check what credentials are currently missing by looking at the environment and the deployment setup.

Actually, looking more carefully at the private_environment_variables, I see GITHUB_TOKEN is listed but it was empty in the previous attempts. Let me use requestEnvs to provide a secure input widget for the user to enter these credentials.

The key credentials needed for the deployment pipeline:
- RENDER_API_KEY - for triggering Render deploys
- RENDER_SERVICE_ID - the Render service to deploy to
- GITHUB_TOKEN - for pushing to GitHub (already exists but may be empty)

Let me provide a secure credential input form.

envs.name: RENDER_API_KEY
envs.description: Render API key from https://dashboard.render.com/u/settings#api-keys — used to trigger deploys and check deploy status

envs.name: RENDER_SERVICE_ID
envs.description: Render service ID (found in the Render dashboard URL: dashboard.render.com/web/srv-XXXXX)

envs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
envs.description: GitHub personal access token with repo scope — for pushing code and triggering workflows

envs.name: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
envs.description: Supabase service role key for backend database access

input.reason: These credentials are required to fix the Render auto-deploy pipeline and complete the end-to-end deployment verification. Without them, the backend cannot authenticate with Render, GitHub, or Supabase.

Without them, the backend cannot authenticate with Render, GitHub, or Supabase."}
